basics.name: Diana Zamora
basics.label: Student
basics.summary: I am studying for a B.S. in Computer Science in the Department of Information and Computer Sciences at the University of Louisville. I expect to graduate in Fall, 2025.

education.institution: Jefferson Community and Techincal College, Louisville Kentucky
Jefferson Community and Techincal College, Louisville Kentucky

highlights: Efficiently lift and handle heavy packages to ensure safe transportation.
Thoroughly secure packages using appropriate methods and equipment.
Collaborate effectively with coworkers to maintain a smooth workflow.
Possess a valid driver's certification for operating company vehicles.
Demonstrated leadership skills as a floater, providing guidance and support to employees.
Regularly handle packages weighing over 70 pounds with care and precision.

work.company: CERC (Committee for Employee Retention and Coaching) - UPS
highlights: Assumed a leadership role as Co-Chair Assistant within the CERC program, supporting the Co-Chairs in their responsibilities.
Managed and organized meetings, ensuring smooth logistics and effective communication among committee members.
Collaborated with the Co-Chairs to develop meeting agendas and facilitate productive discussions.
Assisted in coordinating mentoring activities, matching veteran part-time employees with those in need of guidance.
Played a key role in tracking attendance and training issues among employees, identifying areas for improvement.
Assisted in the implementation of strategies to enhance employee retention and job satisfaction.
Acted as a point of contact for employees seeking guidance or assistance within the program.
Contributed to the development of resources and materials to support employee onboarding and retention efforts.
Provided input and feedback to the Co-Chairs regarding the progress and success of the retention program.
Demonstrated strong organizational skills in managing various administrative tasks related to the committee's operations.
Strategically managed inventory of donated supplies and implemented a budget for spending and donations to ensure efficient allocation of resources.
Collaborated closely with fellow volunteers to organize and coordinate engaging activities and events for youth, fostering a supportive and inclusive environment.
Fostered partnerships with other organizations to plan and execute successful community events, promoting awareness and acceptance.
Demonstrated compassion and sensitivity in providing support to individuals dealing with suicidal/self-mutilation tendencies, offering a nonjudgmental and empathetic approach.
Utilized strong problem-solving skills to effectively address emotional and physical conflicts, offering guidance and finding resolutions to promote a safe and nurturing environment for youth.

awards.title: Dean's List Fall 2022
awards.summary: Recognized for achieving exceptional academic performance during the Fall 2022 semester.
